title: PM Ops Metrics Threshold Evaluation
objective: Implement the smallest bounded follow-on to packet 019j by adding one deployment-side threshold-evaluation path over the existing PM idempotency metrics export payload so scheduled runs can produce an explicit pass/fail operator signal without changing application-runtime code, adding DDL, adding PM write endpoints, or reopening `work.wbs_nodes` writes.
active_role: PM Ops Metrics Threshold Evaluation Author
constrained_outputs: One bounded deployment-side threshold-evaluation artifact that consumes the existing PM idempotency export payload and emits an explicit pass/fail operator signal without adding any application-runtime endpoint, background worker, or database write path
One pinned threshold contract covering only existing exported fields such as total count, expired count, oldest expiry, and the seven per-route rows, without inventing new runtime metrics
One operator runbook documenting the threshold semantics, failure behavior, and how to tune the bounded thresholds without changing the application runtime

Focused validation evidence proving no application-runtime files changed, `/api/v1/work/*` path count remains 15, `/api/v1/ops/*` path count remains 2, and the existing packet-019j export path remains the only data source
validation_steps: Confirm packet 019k stays bounded to deployment-side threshold evaluation over the existing packet-019j export payload and does not modify application-runtime code, add new endpoints, or reopen `work.wbs_nodes` writes
Confirm the threshold path reuses only the existing packet-019j export artifact or payload and does not introduce a second scrape path or a third PM ops endpoint
Confirm no DDL, migration edits, or changes to the `pm.idempotency_keys` schema are introduced

Confirm packet-019g, packet-019h, packet-019i, and packet-019j runtime and observability surfaces remain unchanged and that `/api/v1/work/*` stays at 15 while `/api/v1/ops/*` stays at 2 after threshold evaluation lands
sql_permission_note: Packet 019k is deployment-side only. No new SQL, schema changes, migration edits, runtime route changes, or broader PM write-surface expansion are authorized.
